name: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni
summary: open multimodal model unifying vision, audio, and language → text with 9x higher throughput than other open omni models — weights live on Hugging Face and OpenRouter, also via NVIDIA NIM

name: Xiaomi MiMo-V2.5-Pro
summary: open-source 1.02T MoE (42B active, 1M context, MIT license) that benchmarks alongside Claude Opus 4.6 on coding — full weights and tokenizer on Hugging Face

name: DeepSeek V4
summary: open-source flagship in two sizes (V4-Pro 1.6T params, V4-Flash 284B) with 1M token context and Hybrid Attention — preview weights on Hugging Face, API at $0.28/M tokens for Flash and $3.48/M for Pro

name: GPT-5.5
summary: OpenAI's new flagship — better at coding, deep research, and multi-step agentic tasks; rolling out to Plus/Pro/Business in ChatGPT and Codex, with GPT-5.5 and 5.5 Pro live in the API

name: Google MCP for Google Services
summary: official managed MCP servers for Google Maps, BigQuery, Cloud Run, Compute Engine, and more — plug Google services directly into your AI agent without building your own integrations

name: ChatGPT Images 2.0
summary: new gpt-image-2 model with native reasoning, 2K resolution output, and multi-image consistency — generates product shots, brand visuals, and illustrations that stay coherent across a set

name: Grok STT + TTS APIs
summary: standalone speech-to-text ($0.10/hr batch) and text-to-speech ($4.20/1M chars) APIs — word-level timestamps, speaker diarization, 25+ languages, available immediately to all developers

name: Claude Design
summary: text-to-visual prototyping tool that generates mockups, slides, and one-pagers from prompts — reads your codebase and design files for brand consistency, exports to PDF/PPTX/Canva; available in Claude Pro/Max
